title: quercus breviloba
intro: Quercus breviloba, also known as the Japanese oak or small-leafed oak, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to East Asia, characterized by its small leaves and broad, rounded crown. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Quercus breviloba include reducing fever, relieving rheumatic pain, and improving cardiovascular health, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Quercus breviloba can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
